steps.title: Determine slope direction
steps.content: The scatterplot likely shows a downward trend since two answer choices ($$y=-1.9x±10.1$$) have negative slopes and two ($$y=1.9x±10.1$$) have positive slopes. A negative slope means the line decreases as $x$ increases, which matches the likely negative correlation in the scatterplot.

steps.title: Analyze y-intercept sign
steps.content: When $x=0$, equation B gives $y = -1.9(0) + 10.1 = 10.1$, so it crosses the y-axis at $y=10.1$. Equation A would produce $y = -10.1$ at $x=0$. Observing the scatterplot's trendline position relative to the y-axis confirms it must intersect there above zero.

steps.title: Match to answer choices
steps.content: Combining negative slope requirement (ruling out C and D) with positive y-intercept confirmation (ruling out A) leaves only choice B. That matches choice B — B is correct.
